High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall transfer data as efficiently as possible.

Target allocation code: CS
Target subsystem: Collector Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services shall compress outgoing data payloads using a lossless compression algorithm prior to transmission.
The Collector Services shall aggregate multiple small data packets into a single batch transfer to reduce per-packet overhead.
The Collector Services shall implement connection reuse and pooling for all outgoing data streams to minimize connection establishment latency.
The Collector Services shall support data deduplication by caching recently transmitted data and avoiding retransmission of identical payloads.
The Collector Services shall limit the size of outgoing data chunks to a configurable maximum to prevent fragmentation and ensure efficient network utilization.

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall be able to implement quality checking spatial tests using available data.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Quality Checking Services shall provide a spatial test function that compares observed data values with data from geographically adjacent stations.
The Quality Checking Services shall support configurable spatial interpolation methods for estimating expected values at a station location from surrounding stations.
The Quality Checking Services shall use available real-time and historical observation data from the Environmental Metadata Cache (EMC) as input for spatial tests.
The Quality Checking Services shall calculate spatial consistency metrics such as standard deviation or interquartile range across stations within a defined radius.
The Quality Checking Services shall flag observations as suspect if they deviate from the spatially interpolated value by more than a configurable threshold.

Target allocation code: QChS
Target subsystem: Quality Checking Services

Draft JSON:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Quality Checking Services shall start processing received environmental data within 1 second of data receipt.
The Quality Checking Services shall complete all configured quality checks for a single data record within 9 seconds of starting processing.
The Quality Checking Services shall support concurrent processing of multiple data records without exceeding the 10-second total time limit per record.
The Quality Checking Services shall log the start and end timestamps for each quality check to enable verification of the 10-second requirement.
The Quality Checking Services shall abort processing and report a timeout if a quality check exceeds 10 seconds from data receipt.
